name: Defined chemostat medium, succinate carbon source + nitrate electron acceptor (high-copper, [Cu]=13 umol/L) (PMC3831637)
description: defined chemostat medium, succinate carbon source + nitrate electron acceptor (high-copper, [Cu]=13 umol/L) — medium reported in PMC3831637 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC3831637 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'Paracoccus denitrificans defined minimal chemostat medium (succinate/nitrate, Vishniac & Santer trace elements) as used by Felgate et al. 2012' (Felgate H., Giannopoulos G., Sullivan M.J., Gates A.J., Clarke T.A., Baggs E., Rowley G., Richardson D.J. (2012) 'The impact of copper, nitrate and carbon status on the emission of nitrous oxide by two species of bacteria with biochemically distinct denitrification pathways.' Environmental
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_note: PMC3831637 is a modeling paper on P. denitrificans denitrification; it does NOT contain the defined medium's salts, buffer, vitamins, trace-element list, or carbon source with amounts (these are only cited from Felgate et al. 2012). The medium is carbon-LIMITED; the paper does not name succinate, so the current record's 'succinate carbon source' cannot be verified from this source. Full formulatio
provenance.recipe_evidence: Gaimster et al. 2016 (reproducing the Felgate et al. 2012 medium): defined minimal medium contained '29 mmol/L Na2HPO4, 11 mmol/L KH2PO4, 10 mmol/L NH4Cl, 0.4 mmol/L MgSO4' supplemented with '30 mmol/L sodium succinate, 20 mmol/L NaNO3' and 2 mL/L Vishniac and Santer trace elements solution ['130 mmol/L EDTA, 7.64 mmol/L ZnSO4, 25 mmol/L MnCl2, 18.5 mmol/L FeSO4, 0.89 mmol/L (NH4)6Mo7O24, 6.4 mmol/L CuSO4, 6.72 mmol/L CoCl2, 37.4 mmol/L CaCl2']. Trace-element final concentrations above are the stock values diluted 2 mL/L. PMC3831637 (Woolfenden et al. 2013) models exactly this system (Paracocc
provenance.base_medium: Paracoccus denitrificans defined minimal chemostat medium (succinate carbon / nitrate electron acceptor), phosphate-buffered, with 2 mL/L Vishniac & Santer trace-element solution; anaerobic denitrifying chemostat, D=0.05 h-1. High-copper variant ([Cu]~13 umol/L, full CuSO4 in trace mix).
provenance.base_medium_reference: Felgate H., Giannopoulos G., Sullivan M.J., Gates A.J., Clarke T.A., Baggs E., Rowley G., Richardson D.J. (2012) 'The impact of copper, nitrate and carbon status on the emission of nitrous oxide by two species of bacteria with biochemically distinct denitrification pathways.' Environmental Microbiology 14(7):1788-1800. PMID: 22642644, DOI: 10.1111/j.1462-2920.2012.02789.x. Full composition reproduced verbatim in Gaimster et al. 2016 Front. Microbiol. 7:1806 (DOI 10.3389/fmicb.2016.01806) and following Sullivan et al. 2013.
provenance.verification_evidence: the defined minimal medium contained 29 mmol/L Na2HPO4, 11 mmol/L KH2PO4, 10 mmol/L NH4Cl, 0.4 mmol/L MgSO4

sources_chased.what: Target paper PMC3831637 (Woolfenden et al. 2013, MicrobiologyOpen) - a modeling paper; states chemostat conditions but defers medium composition to Felgate et al. 2012
Base medium as cited to Felgate et al. 2012 (Environ Microbiol 14:1788, PMID 22642644) - primary source; full text paywalled (HTTP 403 on PNAS/Wiley)
Gaimster et al. 2016 (Front. Microbiol. 7:1806) - same Richardson/Gates/Rowley lab, reproduces the identical defined medium verbatim (base salts + full Vishniac & Santer trace-element composition)
added: full Vishniac & Santer trace metals (Zn,Mn,Fe,Mo,Cu,Co,Ca) with stock concentrations

recuration.notes: Recipe recovered from Gaimster et al. 2016, which reproduces verbatim the same defined P. denitrificans succinate/nitrate chemostat medium used by Felgate et al. 2012 (the base-medium reference cited by the modeling paper PMC3831637). All 12 trace/salt concentrations already in the record match a 500x (2 mL/L) dilution of the Vishniac & Santer stock exactly. The two previously uncovered compounds (Na2HPO4 buffer, EDTA chelator) are confirmed real and now included. This is the high-copper variant (full CuSO4 = 12.8 umol/L ~ the paper's 13 umol/L); the low-copper variant instead omits/chelates C
